finding_results.notes: Anya's surveillance post is made physically legible before the reveal, she states the west-side corridor brought her here to watch Dominic's gate, she identifies the immediate reporting risk to Yor, and Yor's skipped confirmation window turns the encounter into an on-page decision with operational consequences.

Dominic's own signature schedule and buyer protocol create the limited window; repeated intercom and radio pressure tighten the search; Yor only captures priority pages and assignment headers before the hallway closes on her; the delayed exit costs her the clean exfiltration route and causes the razor-wire injury.
The opening sentence now anchors Chapter 10 as the next evening after the safe-house dawn and clarifies why the chapter is leaving that thread.

The chapter now bridges Damian's extraction and Anya's narrowed corridor in the opening, then cashes it out at the canal through the WS-7-marked observation post and Anya's admission that the west-side line ended at Dominic's gate.
Chapter word count is 5523 while preserving the required heading contract.

Revised four tail-end responses in the canal exchange where Anya had collapsed into neutral monosyllables. Line 251: 'Yes' expanded to 'Yes. It was just watching. I wasn't going in.' — tangential honesty deflecting from the actual question (alone?) to a different truth (just surveillance). Line 257: 'I'm fine, Mama' replaced with 'I know the route. I've got layers.' — answers the safety concern underneath the coat question rather than the surface question, revealing operational familiarity with the area. Line 261: 'Okay' replaced with 'I'll come by. Tomorrow, maybe.' — Anya's natural directness producing commitment instead of noncommittal filler. Line 265: 'I'll try it' expanded to 'It's probably not that bad. I'll try it.' — over-quick reassurance about the salt worry before Yor has fully landed her apology, consistent with Anya's pattern of responding faster than the conversation warrants. These join the already-strong earlier lines (line 221 self-correction, line 229 excess operational knowledge, line 233 concealment-pressure directive) to produce an exchange where Anya is identifiable from speech alone throughout. No line in the revised exchange reads as interchangeable generic filial dialogue.

finding_results.notes: Regrounded Yor's moral break in her own operational and domestic lexicon: briefing paperwork, return codes, fifteenth-of-the-month deposits, a bodily tilt in the room, the clerk's invoices, and the remembered closeout slips she accepted without inspection.
Bond now enters through Yor's household observation of how he occupies the rug, breathes, and gets stepped around, rather than through repeated size/age certification.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 10 now orients itself against the safe-house dawn immediately, gives Yor's records discovery a concrete timer and exfiltration cost, makes Anya's parallel surveillance mission legible on the page, and ends with Yor's first active break from Garden procedure after seeing her daughter at Dominic's wall.

Single finding addressed. Four generic monosyllabic Anya responses in the canal scene's domestic tail were replaced with lines carrying character-specific concealment pressure: tangential honesty, operational-knowledge slips, blunt commitment, and over-quick reassurance. The stronger Anya-specific dialogue earlier in the exchange (self-correction at line 221, excess knowledge at line 229, the 'don't make whatever call comes next' directive at line 233) was preserved unchanged. Scene restraint and cover integrity maintained — no confessional material added.

Addressed all packet findings with local chapter edits: Bond's introduction now comes through Yor-specific domestic noticing, the targeted prose-template repetitions were replaced with more direct language, and Yor's institutional break is rendered through concrete paperwork, bodily reaction, and remembered procedure rather than abstract summary.
